sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The cursor moved right again - now it's showing two blue pixels side by side (columns 35 and 36). The pattern seems to be:
- Stage 12: cursor at col 35 (1 pixel)
- Stage 13: cursor at col 35 (1 pixel) - wait, that doesn't match
Let me reconsider. Looking at the images:
- After stage 11: cursor was at col ~35, single pixel
- After stage 12: cursor moved left to col ~34
- After stage 13: cursor moved right to cols 35-36 (2 pixels)
The cursor is oscillating but seems to be expanding. Maybe the level completes when the cursor reaches a certain width or position. Let me continue with more diamond patterns.
Actually, I've been doing this for many stages now (14+ stages) and the level hasn't completed. The efficiency budget says I should have ~36 actions but I'm at 74. I need to find a faster way or a different approach.
Let me think about what I might be missing:
1. Maybe the level has a different completion condition
2. Maybe I need to click on something else after the cursor reaches a certain position
3. Maybe the pattern needs to be different
Let me try clicking on the right end of the bar or the green strip to see if that triggers completion. Or maybe I need to click when the cursor is at a specific position.
Actually, let me re-examine the bar. The bar has:
- Left blue square (the "start" marker)
- Gray bar
- Blue cursor that moves
- Right end of bar
Maybe when the cursor reaches the right end, I need to click on it or on the right end to complete. Let me try clicking on the rightmost part of the bar.
Let me first check the current cursor position more precisely.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current cursor position and bar structure
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['b', 'S']:  # blue or sky blue
        print(f"Color={n['color']}, id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# The cursor is oscillating. Let me try clicking on the far right end of the bar
# to see if that triggers level completion when cursor is near the end.
# Based on boundaries, the bar ends around col 40-42
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 20, 'col': 40}])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
